concepts.name: Contact card v1 binary format (header/version/flags, encode/decode/parse)
CARD_CONTENT canonical signed-content string, shared by encode and decode
parseContactCard is the single decode seam for every npub entry point
importCard -> writeContactEntry adapter (ISO updatedAt derivation, avatar preservation)
contactCache last-writer-wins by lexical ISO updatedAt comparison
qr.ts scan-payload discrimination (bare npub vs. card payload)
Add Contact flow populates nickname from an imported card
Share action encodes the full onboarding URL (not bare payload) at ECC-L, cached until nickname change
/add page reads the card exclusively from window.location.hash (#c=), never a query param or server round trip
Profile nickname save enforces the 32-UTF-8-byte cap (source-of-truth, not just encode-side backstop)
Group-invite-by-card down-converts to pubkeyHex only; never writes contactCache or contacts/knownPeers before the MLS commit lands (DD 8)
Every user-facing string added by this epic has both en and de entries in i18n.ts

concepts_realized: profile nickname 32-UTF-8-byte cap enforced at ProfileContext.saveProfile chokepoint + storage normalize backstop
observations: Review caught a SECOND save path (settings.tsx identity-restore) bypassing the input cap; fixed by moving enforcement to the saveProfile chokepoint which caps in-memory state (MLS broadcast source) + storage for every caller.
storage.normalizeUserProfile now byte-caps (was char-slice) as persistence backstop for restored/legacy profiles.
Wording: user-facing notice says "32-character limit (accented letters and emoji count for more)" while counter shows bytes — accepted as product copy (sev3, non-blocking).

observations: RESOLVED-BY-S5 [was sev5]: NpubQrScanner is now SINGLE-OWNER of the scan seam but SHARED by AddContactModal (card-aware) and InviteMemberModal. Swapping to normaliseScanPayload widened accepted input for the invite scanner too -> a card QR pre-filled a card link the invite submit rejected as invalid_npub. Closed by S5 making handleInvite route through parseContactCard (same delivery). DD-8 safe.
S6 (settings.tsx) uses NpubQrModal in display mode only (no onScan) -> onScan widening is backward-compatible for it.

processContactInput maps all parse failures (incl. signature failure) to 'invalid_npub' copy — AC-PARSE-4 satisfied, but message conflates integrity failure with malformed identifier (sev2, unactioned Review note).

FIXED (Codex P2): parseContactCard branched on a lowercase 'npub1' prefix only, regressing uppercase NPUB1… (QR alphanumeric mode) which the old normaliseNpubPayload path accepted via nip19.decode. Made the npub branch case-insensitive in contactCard.ts + added AC-PARSE-1 uppercase regression test.

observations: DD-8 verified: InviteMemberModal imports NO write helper (no importCard/contactCache/knownPeers); resolveInviteTarget uses only pubkeyHex, discards profile. MarmotContext.inviteByNpub byte-for-byte unchanged.

AC-GRP-2 parity test judged ADEQUATE (Opus + examiner concur): S5's only influence on the KeyPackage dependency is the npub handed to the unchanged inviteByNpub; parity test proves card-vs-bare-npub produce identical (groupId,npub) args + result. Pre-existing gap: no real MarmotContext no_key_package unit test → non-blocking backlog follow-up.

FIXED (examiner sev4): nostr:npub paste regressed (parseContactCard didn't strip nostr:; old normaliseNpubPayload did). Fixed at the single seam in contactCard.ts + regression test; retroactively repairs S4's paste path too.

resolveInviteTarget (S5) and processContactInput (S4) share the parse->invalid_npub->pubkeyToNpub shape but are NOT a duplicated invariant — decode invariant single-owned by parseContactCard; each is a thin adapter (S4 keeps profile, S5 discards it).

concepts_realized: Share action encodes the full onboarding URL (not bare payload) at ECC-L, cached until nickname change
concepts_consumed: parseContactCard is the single decode seam for every npub entry point
observations: PRIVACY VERIFIED (AC-SEC-1), independently by Opus + examiner + Codex: the entire share path emits zero relay events — encodeCard SIGNS (no publish), buildShareUrl is a string op, NpubQrModal renders QR locally + clipboard copy. NIP-46 sign is a round trip to the user's OWN bunker (key custody), not a public-relay broadcast.

FIXED (examiner sev6, impl_bug): share-card cache key omitted pubkeyHex, so a mid-session identity restore (no reload) with matching empty-nickname+local-signer returned the PREVIOUS identity's card without re-signing. Added pubkeyHex to ShareCardCacheKey + shouldRebuildShareCard + settings source-from-context; two regression tests (predicate + orchestration identity-switch).

FIXED (reviewer sev4, comment rot, privacy-critical): settings.tsx ~L459 claimed replaceIdentity publishes kind-0; it does NOT (NostrIdentityContext.tsx:384-394, privacy invariant). Corrected comment; no runtime change.

DEFERRED to epic e2e gate (examiner VQ-S6-007, ac_id=AC-UX-4): AC-UX-4 prose mandates a local-mode e2e; S6 scope.excludes it and assigns it to the epic e2e step. Tracked as a Step 5.8 e2e-gate obligation, not an S6 blocker.

concepts_consumed: parseContactCard is the single decode seam for every npub entry point
Add Contact flow populates nickname from an imported card
importCard -> writeContactEntry adapter (ISO updatedAt derivation, avatar preservation)
observations: PRIVACY VERIFIED (AC-SEC-1/DD-9), Opus + examiner + Codex: card read only from window.location.hash inside useEffect; no useRouter/query-param/server-visible path; fragment never leaves the browser. Both AC-UX-7 modes traced.

AC-UX-7 card survival is by construction: the card lives in the URL hash (never mutated), re-read when hydrated flips true; settledRef (StrictMode-safe) + synchronous processContactInput prevent double-add. hydrated⇒pubkeyHex non-null (context batches setIdentity+setHydrated).

WHOLE-EPIC SEAM PASS (S1-S7): single decode seam coherent — all entry points route through parseContactCard; importCard called only by processContactInput; group-invite (S5) correctly abstains (DD-8); /add reuses processContactInput. No cross-story contradiction.

FIXED (reviewer sev3): addPage.i18n.test.ts docstring overstated its REQUIRED_KEYS list as git-diff-derived not-hand-fixed. Corrected to describe it as a maintained list (repo precedent) + durability caveat. AC-INTL-1 met for this epic; whole-i18n programmatic walk rejected (would fail on pre-existing out-of-scope divergence). Durability gap → curator backlog follow-up.
ACCEPTED (examiner+Codex sev3): explicit captured-payload ref is optional hardening over the spec-sanctioned retained-hash mechanism → curator backlog follow-up.
